reviews.comment: Professor Potts is a very tough grader, good luck getting above a 95% on anything. ENG 309 was her first online class and she made many mistakes in grading; grades on assignments were changed several times due to her "mess ups." If you needed to turn something in late, she was generally caring and understanding if you email her ahead of time.

This course had some of my favorite texts so I was very disappointed to not like the professor's grading style. Professor Potts is a very nit-picky grader but provides little feedback on what she doesn't like and how students could improve. I am pretty good at grammar/punctuation/formatting but she docks an extreme amount of points for 1-2 errors.

She is great at giving feedback and is clear about the grading rubrics. The content was engaging and worthwhile. She spaced out the work well enough where you were not overwhelmed. A lot of reading and writing but that's what you sign up for. Very understanding about extensions and great at clarifying any miscommunications. She was amazing.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: They didn't have an option but I took 309 English with her she's awful. I get good grades in every English course I do but of course my work isn't good enough for her. She has no openness at all to something different other than her outdated ways of teaching and thinking. Her feedback has no merit and does not make sense. Don't take her !
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Her grading for important assignments (including midterm or final) is mostly vague and refuses to go deeper in her feedback for your grades. She docks points for including anything that she disagrees with and grades you based on subjectivity rather than objectivity. A lot of students from my class had issues with this. However, she is respectful.
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She uses Critical Kendi Consciousness in her courses and she dismisses what students say. The instructors are not very open-minded to student feedback. I get the sense that everyone is plugging along like paper cut out dolls, you know all the same and intersectionally docile.
-- Zero percent, that is 0%, that is zero, would take this again. This form does not even ask if I would take course again. Not 100%

-- Awful. 

-- 100% would not waste money here. 

-- if I write specifics RMP erases it.

Professor Potts is very sweet and loves a good discussion. Sometimes it's hard to interpret her intent, but if you are engaged and ask questions then you won't get lost. Read the readings, write the papers and show up, you'll do great!
reviews.ratingTags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Donna Potts is one of the most wholesome, down to Earth professors I've ever had. Always willing to share personal anecdotes with the class, she makes sure everyone gets the help they need. One of the best professors at WSU.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Inspirational
